messages.content:
In respect of your HK tax briefing at 10:30am this morning, we apologize that we will be joining the meeting about 10 minutes late due to urgent meeting. 

check please

In respect of your HK tax briefing at 10:30am this morning, we apologize for the delay in joining the meeting. We had an urgent meeting that caused us to be approximately 10 minutes late. Thank you and please let us know if we missed any crucial information.
we will join the meeting 10 mins late,
Thank you for letting us know. We will begin the briefing promptly at 10:30am and you can join us when you are able to. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out to us.

As discussed, please find below the Zoom meeting dial in details for our call at 2:30pm later this afternoon. 
check please

As we've discussed earlier, please find the Zoom meeting dial-in details below for our scheduled call at 2:30 pm later this afternoon. 
[Insert Zoom meeting ID and password]. 
If you have any trouble accessing the call, please let us know and we will be happy to provide assistance. Thank you and looking forward to speaking with you soon.

Post delayed due to insufficient postage

check please

Your post has been delayed and will not be delivered due to insufficient postage. Please ensure that the correct amount of postage is applied to your mail before sending it to avoid further delay. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact your local postal service for assistance.
just check the sentence

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It informs the recipient that their mail has been delayed due to insufficient postage and advises them to ensure that they apply the correct amount of postage on their mail in the future.

Long outstanding case, STA Inbound departed in Oct 2022, never respond to our follow up until now.

check sentence

The sentence has a couple of issues in terms of grammar and the use of dates:

"Long outstanding case, STA inbound departed in October 2022, and we have not received a response to our follow-up until now."

The sentence above is clearer and more concise. The word "long" provides context for the case, and we ensure that all the important details are included such as the date of the STA inbound departure and our lack of response until the present time.

Further to the below email, we understand that you would like to schedule your HK tax briefing with Gigi on  22 June 2023 (Thursday) at 11:00AM India time. However, please note that next Thursday will be HK public holiday. Your HK tax briefing will need to be rescheduled. 

Kindly let us know your availability by providing 3 time slots within the next two weeks so that we can schedule a suitable time to discuss with you.

check please

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It informs the recipient that due to a public holiday in Hong Kong, their HK tax briefing scheduled for June 22, 2023, needs to be rescheduled. It then requests the recipient to provide three time slots within the next two weeks for rescheduling. However, I would suggest a minor change to the first sentence by rephrasing it as:

"Following up on our previous email, we understand that you would like to schedule your HK tax briefing with Gigi on June 22, 2023, at 11:00 AM India time. However, please note that next Thursday is a public holiday in Hong Kong, and we need to reschedule your HK tax briefing." 

This change will create a clearer connection between the previous email and the current one and also make the sentence flow more smoothly.

Kindly let us know your availability by providing 3 time slots within the next two weeks so that we can schedule a suitable time to discuss with you.

check this too

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It politely requests the recipient to provide three available time slots within the next two weeks in order to facilitate the scheduling of a new time for their HK tax briefing. Overall, the sentence is well-written and effectively conveys the action required by the recipient.

Thank you for your reply and well noted you have handled the instalment matters. 

Please note that the attached Tax Representative Appointment Letter authorizes us to act on your behalf when it comes to your HK salaries tax related matters. Without the letter, we are unable to liaise with the HK Inland Revenue Department (“IRD”) on your behalf. As such, please sign and return the letter for our submission to the IRD. 

check please

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It thanks the recipient for their response and acknowledges that the instalment matters have been handled. It then reminds the recipient that the attached Tax Representative Appointment Letter authorizes the sender to act on their behalf with regards to their HK salaries tax related matters. It also states that without this letter, the sender is unable to liaise with the HK Inland Revenue Department on their behalf. Finally, it requests the recipient to sign and return the letter for submission to the IRD. Overall, the sentence is well-written and conveys the necessary information effectively.

Please also note that we have double checked the available information and we are unable to locate any of the below information regarding DOA and the details of the residence provided. We would appreciate if you could provide the following for us to assist with your HK tax filings:

1.	Please advise if you have received Daily Overseas Allowance ("DOA") during your STA outside HK?  If yes, could you please advise the amount of DOA per day? 
2.	Please also advise if HSBC has provided you place of residence during your STA outside HK. If yes, could you please let us know the address (if known) and nature of housing (e.g. flat, serviced apartment, 1-room hotel, 2-room hotel, etc.)?

check this too

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It advises the recipient that the sender has double-checked the available information but cannot locate the requested information regarding DOA and the details of residence provided. It then makes a polite request for additional information to assist with the recipient's HK tax filings. The request is structured in two parts: the first asking for information on whether the recipient received a Daily Overseas Allowance (DOA) during their STA outside HK, and if so, the amount of DOA per day. The second part asks for information on whether HSBC provided the recipient with a place of residence during their STA outside HK, and if so, requests the address, if known, and nature of the housing. Overall, the sentence is well-written and effectively conveys the information requested while maintaining a polite and professional tone.

We note that the provided time slots (i.e. 5:00pm – 6:00pm IST) will be 7:30pm – 8:30pm HK time. As such, we would appreciate it if you could provide earlier time slots for our arrangement. 
check sentence

The sentence is grammatically correct and clear in its message. It politely acknowledges the provided time slots requested by the recipient and informs them that these times would be outside Hong Kong's working hours. As such, the sender requests for earlier time slots for rescheduling of the call. Overall, the sentence is well-written and effectively conveys the message while maintaining a polite and professional tone.
please rephrase

"Certainly, we have noted the time slots you provided (i.e. 5:00 pm - 6:00 pm IST) however, we would like to draw your attention to the fact that these times would be outside working hours in Hong Kong (i.e., 7:30 pm – 8:30 pm HK time). We would greatly appreciate it if you could suggest earlier time slots for rescheduling the call." 

This rephrased version maintains the same meaning and tone while making the sentence flow more smoothly.
rephrase again with shorter message
"We noted the time slots you provided (5:00 pm - 6:00 pm IST). However, these times would be outside of Hong Kong's working hours. Could you suggest earlier time slots for rescheduling the call?"
please include (i.e., 7:30 pm – 8:30 pm HK time)

"Thanks for providing the time slots (5:00 pm - 6:00 pm IST). However, please note that these times (i.e., 7:30 pm – 8:30 pm HK time) fall outside of our working hours. Would you be able to suggest earlier time slots for rescheduling the call?"

Thanks for providing the time slots (5:00 pm - 6:00 pm IST). 

However, please note that these times (i.e., 7:30 pm – 8:30 pm HK time) fall outside of our working hours. Please would you be able to suggest earlier time slots for our arrangement for the briefing? Thanks.

how about this? better?
Yes, that works well! It maintains a polite and professional tone while conveying the necessary information in a concise manner.
better or worse ?

Both versions are good. The previous version was a bit shorter, while the revised version provides additional context by including the phrase "for our arrangement for the briefing." Overall, both versions convey the same message effectively.
